extraction.one_line_summary: POLITT Nils soloed to victory from 12km out after escaping a 13-rider breakaway on an uncategorized climb inside 15km.
tactical_patterns: Bora sacrificed Sagan DNS but salvaged team morale by driving echelons early then committing Politt to breakaway.
World champion Alaphilippe in 13-rider break signals UAE/Deceuninck willing to burn cards on ambiguous transition stages.
Politt's winning move on uncategorized climb at 12km proves breakaway selection matters more than categorized summit positioning.

rider_signals.POLITT Nils: positive: First-year Bora signing immediately repaid classics pedigree with sustained 12km solo, suggesting undervalued stage-hunting threat.
rider_signals.ERVITI Imanol: positive: 17-year domestique nearly won from break, revealing Movistar's depth riders have autonomy on non-GC days.
rider_signals.SWEENEY Harry: positive: Tour debutant podiumed from elite 13-rider break, signaling dangerous late-race positioning instincts.
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: neutral: World champion made selection but faded on uncategorized climb, suggesting form not yet peaked mid-tour.
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: negative: Caught in group three/four during echelons despite no major splits, showing Sky/Ineos vulnerability without full train.
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: positive: Green jersey points from peloton arrival confirms he's hunting classification through consistency, not stage wins alone.
